bodyFields.claim: Subjective experience cannot be reduced to objective physical descriptions because any shift to greater objectivity takes us farther from its real nature.
bodyFields.support: In understanding external phenomena like lightning, we can move from a subjective viewpoint to a more objective one and still refer to the same thing. However, experience is essentially tied to a particular point of view; abandoning it loses the phenomenon. The reduction of the external world succeeds only by omitting species-specific viewpoints, but mental states require that viewpoint as part of their essence. Therefore, no present conception gives a clue how to account for subjective character in physical terms.

bodyFields.definition: Objectivity is not an endpoint but a direction in which understanding can travel, moving away from a strictly human viewpoint towards more accurate views of real nature.
bodyFields.significance: It allows describing phenomena in terms of general effects and properties not dependent on human senses, but it fails when applied to subjective experience because it takes us farther from the real nature of the phenomenon.

bodyFields.definition: The what-it-is-like aspect of an experience that is fully comprehensible only from one point of view, the perspective of the subject.
